steps.title: Find Defoe's main contrast
steps.content: Defoe contrasts a sermon, which lasts only as far as memory can carry it, with a printed book, which remains available as a record and can convey its contents for ages.

steps.title: Reject reversals and tangents
steps.content: Choice B reverses the passage by making speech easier to remember than print. Choice C introduces expert delivery, which is never mentioned. Choice D focuses on whether authors are remembered, not on the endurance of printed words. The answer is A.
